title: Sales Person
description:
We are looking for a Sales Person who is experienced but at the same time willing to learn and listen to new sales techniques.



The company you’ll be representing is a well-established catering company based in Maryland, known for delivering exceptional culinary experiences and outstanding service. You’ll work closely with the CEO and will be trained by her on how to win deals.



Responsibilities:

++ Identify and pursue new sales opportunities through inbound leads, referrals, and outbound outreach

++ Build and maintain strong, long-term customer relationships

++ Prepare and deliver persuasive presentations and proposals

++ Bring potential customers through the sales process and close contracts to meet or exceed sales targets

++ Track and manage sales activity using CRM tools



Qualifications:

>> Proven experience in sales with a strong record of closing deals

>> Excellent communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills

>> Positive, coachable attitude and a willingness to adopt new strategies

>> Highly organized, self-motivated, and goal-driven

>> Must be available to work Eastern Time (ET) hours



If you’re an experienced salesperson who’s open to learning new techniques and enjoys helping clients bring their events to life, we’d love to hear from you. Please include "I am your deal maker" in the subject line of your response.



Also, please answer the following questions:

1. What types of companies have you supported as a Sales Person?

2. Based on your answer in number 1, what were your responsibilities?

3. Please share an example of a time when you successfully closed a major deal.

4. For you, what is the hardest part of being a Sales Person?

5. How much is your hourly rate?

6. Can you commit in working ET hours?

title: Executive Appointment Setter/Closer (High-Performance Sales Role | American Accent Required)
description:
Executive Appointment Setter / Closer (High-Performance Sales Role | American Accent Required)



We are hiring a high-caliber Executive Appointment Setter with a proven track record of closed-won sales, not just booking appointments. This is a performance-driven sales role with a clear pathway into advanced sales execution through structured training.



This is NOT a beginner role. We are looking for someone who can think, execute, and convert with minimal supervision.



Position Details

Role: Executive Appointment Setter (Sales Performance Role)

Type: Part-Time (with potential for long-term expansion)

Schedule: Monday–Friday, 10:30 AM – 3:00 PM EST (includes 30-minute unpaid break)

Base Pay: $400 – $600/month (depending on experience)

Commission: 5% on closed-won sales

Leads: Provided (with occasional support for lead generation if needed)

Role Overview



You will be responsible for handling high-quality sales conversations, qualifying prospects, and booking appointments with strong conversion intent.



This role requires someone who is not only able to set appointments but can also demonstrate real sales ability and closing experience.



You must be confident, intelligent in conversation, and capable of operating independently with minimal guidance.



Key Responsibilities

Handle inbound and outbound sales conversations using provided leads

Qualify prospects based on defined criteria and sales fit

Book high-quality appointments with strong attendance rates

Maintain CRM accuracy, pipeline tracking, and lead status updates

Execute structured follow-ups to maximize conversions

Occasionally assist in lead generation tasks if required

Maintain consistent, professional, and persuasive communication across all channels

Performance Standards (Critical)

Must have a 100% American accent and natural American tone (non-negotiable)

Must demonstrate a verified track record of closed-won sales

Must operate with high-level initiative and independent decision-making ability

Must require minimal mentorship to close deals effectively

Must think strategically, handle objections intelligently, and move conversations toward conversion without step-by-step instruction

Must consistently perform with high accountability, ownership, and results-driven execution

Training & Growth Path



This role includes a structured Sales Mastery Training Program focused on:



Appointment setting excellence

Advanced objection handling

Sales psychology and conversion strategy



Upon successful completion and demonstrated mastery, candidates will transition into advanced sales closing responsibilities within the organization.



Progression is strictly performance-based, not time-based.



Requirements

Proven experience in appointment setting and/or closing sales with actual wins

Strong English communication skills with a neutral/US-native level accent

Experience using CRM systems and sales pipelines

Ability to work independently and manage time effectively

Strong emotional intelligence and persuasive communication ability

High discipline, consistency, and execution speed

What We Are NOT Looking For

Beginners or entry-level appointment setters

Script readers with no closing experience

Candidates who require heavy supervision

Non-American accent profiles

How to Apply (Mandatory Requirements)



Please submit ALL of the following:



Updated CV or Portfolio (must include relevant sales experience and closed-won results)

Video Introduction (2–3 minutes)

Introduce yourself

Explain your sales experience

Highlight actual closed-won deals and how you achieved them

Explain why you believe you are a high-level executor in sales

 Address

WhatsApp Number

Subject Line: Executive Appointment Setter – [Your Name]

Important



Only applicants who meet the above requirements will be reviewed. This is a high-performance sales position focused on results, not activity.

title: Experienced Brand Manager Assistant to support 12+ Amazon brands for growth, execution, drive performance and operation
description:
Role Overview



We're hiring a Brand Manager Assistant who will work directly under the Senior Brand Manager to execute day-to-day brand operations. This is a hands-on role focused on the “legwork” that drives performance: optimizing listings, managing keyword research, coordinating creative projects, and pulling actionable data.



If the Senior Brand Manager sets the playbook, the Associate Brand Manager runs the plays — ensuring everything gets executed on time, correctly, and at a high level.



Key Responsibilities

1. Listing Management & Optimization

Create, update, and fully optimize Amazon product listings (titles, bullets, descriptions, images, videos, A+ content).

Conduct keyword research and competitor analysis to improve rankings and visibility.

Ensure all listings remain Rufus and Cosmos compliant with Amazon’s latest requirements.

Test content variations (A/B testing) to improve CTR and conversions.



2. Data Research & Analysis

Pull performance data from Amazon Seller Central and external tools (Data Dive, Helium 10, Jungle Scout).

Identify trends, keyword shifts, and competitor activity.

Provide actionable recommendations to the Senior Brand Manager to improve conversions, CTR, and sales velocity.



3. Cross-Team Collaboration

Work closely with the graphic design team to request, coordinate, and deliver images, infographics, and A+ creatives.

Partner with the marketing team to support product launches, promotions, and review-generation initiatives.

Ensure all projects are aligned with deadlines, strategy, and brand guidelines.



4. Conversion-Focused Execution

Continuously update and refine listings to improve conversion rates and customer engagement.

Maintain Amazon Brand Stores, A+ content, and ad creatives for consistency and performance.

Assist in preparing assets for influencer campaigns and external marketing pushes.



5. Project & Compliance Support

Own the administrative “heavy lifting” of flat files, bulk uploads, and catalog troubleshooting.

Flag compliance issues, unauthorized sellers, or listing errors for quick resolution.

Support market expansion efforts by preparing research and draft listings for new product opportunities.



Skills & Qualifications



2+ years experience in Amazon FBA operations, brand management, or digital marketing.

Proficient in Amazon Seller Central, flat files, listing creation, and troubleshooting.

Strong skills in SEO, keyword research, and listing optimization.

Data-driven mindset; confident in Google Sheets and reporting.

Strong communication skills to work cross-functionally with design, marketing, and operations teams.

Highly organized, detail-oriented, and capable of managing multiple deadlines.



What Success Looks Like

Every listing across assigned brands is fully optimized, compliant, and performing.

Senior Brand Manager receives clear, accurate reports and recommendations.

Creative assets are delivered on time because you coordinated smoothly with design.



Product launches happen seamlessly with marketing and review strategies in place.

Conversions and rankings consistently improve due to your execution. If you feel you are the right fit, please respond to the job with the title " The Best Supporting Branding Manager Out There!"



Please respond directly to :

----------



Kind Regards,

Tam Ma

Co Founder

Prodigy Media
